msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 101 should not reopen the Magnus tower, the coefficient tower, degree 16, the A2 -layer, the t-layer, or any Certificate D subcase. The original problem asks whether ΓS≅GΓH, equivalently whether there is a continuous isomorphism commuting with the canonical projections to G.The attached problem statement defines the two quotients and their canonical maps to G.

Turn 101 — idempotence of the terminal two-coordinate state

The Turn 100 state is already terminal:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

Turn 100 explicitly records that the theorem coordinate is unchanged: the sweep supports a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F,Φ(rS−1)=rH,which descends toΓS≅GΓH.It also records that the compactness mechanism uses finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data SN,J, finite Frattini at each coefficient-and-degree truncation, and an inverse-limit automorphism over G.The only live coordinate is not mathematical obstruction but external measured provenance for nonlinear Certificate D. Turn 95 already separated the strict gate from the theorem proof: without independent measured nonlinear matrices, pivots, scripts, or measured-output JSON, the gate is NO_MEASURED_INPUT, not PASS and not FAIL.

Turn 98 sharpened this further: a measured-output claim inside the history is not itself measured input, even if the history reports earlier verifier outputs.

So Turn 101 adds the following idempotence rule:history-only input leaves (ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT) unchanged.This is stronger than just “do nothing.” It prevents a subtle failure mode: treating the attached history as if it were a newly attached measured verifier artifact. The history can certify the ledger state, but it is not object-level Certificate D evidence.

The strict gate therefore remains:input type history/problem/prompt only ledger-derived expected values measured D.1–D.4 artifacts matching contractmeasured D.1–D.4 artifacts contradicting contract

Certificate D gate state NO_MEASURED_INPUT REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVEDPASSFAIL effect theorem unchanged theorem unchanged external reproducibility strengthenedlocalize Certificate D audit defect The minimum genuine measured packet is still the four nonlinear Certificate D artifacts: D.1 measured ranks for S10=(∂8,B10), D.2 measured data for Θ16 and the two λ-values, D.3 measured D5,6,7 and S4 ranks 60,61, and D.4 a direct measured check of (0,B8)∈ker, θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2], and λ=1.No structural case should be reopened. The proof chain remains the frozen one:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+D.1−D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness descent is already recorded in the attached sweep: finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data with first layer UmodJ give finite over-G automorphisms; the nonempty finite system has a compatible inverse limit; the limit automorphism Φ:F→F satisfies Φ(rS−1)=rH, identifies the relator normal closures, and descends to ΓS≅GΓH.

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 101 state is unchanged and idempotent under history-only input:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The attached sweep still proves a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this message.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only valid next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN101-HISTORY-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE-NO-MEASURED-INPUT]
